questions.question: An AI-powered diagnostic tool is found to have a high accuracy rate in detecting breast cancer, however, it has been shown to have a bias towards identifying cancer in women. What are the ethical implications of this finding and how should the developers of this tool address this issue?

A healthcare organization is considering implementing an AI-powered chatbot to assist with patient consultations. The chatbot is programmed to provide accurate and up-to-date information, but it may not be able to handle complex or sensitive patient issues. What are the potential risks and benefits of implementing this system, and how can the organization ensure that it is used in an ethical and responsible manner?

A patient requests that their medical records be accessed and analyzed by an external AI system. The patient believes that this will lead to more accurate diagnoses and better patient outcomes. However, this would require the patient's records to be shared with a third-party organization without their consent. Is this action justified, and what ethical principles should guide the patient's decision-making process?
